spec_match.rationale: Removal feature — page covers one slice (probe / migration / note) but the spec's full removal+replacement story is split across multiple concepts; this page can't carry it alone.

interactive.rationale: Has 2 interactive control(s) wired to JS but the surface is thin — a single button or readout is barely past the static-card threshold.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates the feature-index blurb's key promise (overlap on broadcastchannel, chrome, cross, indexeddb).

edge_cases.rationale: Some fallback / error handling is present but corner cases like removed-element behaviour, denied permission flow, or no-support browsers aren't all covered.

references.rationale: No chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link on the concept page — CLAUDE.md rule #3 says every page must carry the self-heal reference, not rely on the parent feature index.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from the shared CSS variables and the layout follows the stage + controls editorial pattern.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: selectURL and worklet patterns that disappear
openQuestions.detail: The deprecate-and-remove-shared-storage-api feature's headline framing — Shared Storage API removal — cross-site write-only storage goes away — calls out "selectURL and worklet patterns that disappear". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: what replaces opaque cross-site signals
openQuestions.detail: The deprecate-and-remove-shared-storage-api feature's headline framing — Shared Storage API removal — cross-site write-only storage goes away — calls out "what replaces opaque cross-site signals". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Add real controls so the demo isn't a styled code card
openQuestions.detail: Concept pages are supposed to be hands-on. With only 2 button(s), 0 input(s), 0 select(s), 0 range slider(s), the surface is thin. Add at least one slider, picker, or interactive readout so visitors can actually probe the API from the page.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com/feature link to this page
openQuestions.detail: Per CLAUDE.md rule #3, every concept page must include the https://chromestatus.com/feature/5076349064708096 link directly — it is the self-heal anchor used by the slug-recovery script. Right now the page links only back to the parent feature index.

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one realistic failure mode
openQuestions.detail: Add feature detection at the top of the script and surface the result. Handle the permission-denied / API-rejection case in the catch branch with a visible message. If the feature is flag-gated or origin-trial, show explicitly what the page does when the API is absent.

summary: shared-storage-probe under deprecate-and-remove-shared-storage-api: 2 pass / 3 partial / 1 fail. Interactive surface is thin, design system clean, spec story is partial — see open questions for the angles still missing.
